ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jul-24-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-09-292920 Conservatorship of Jerrold Haratani and
PTR-09-292994 The Maria Miyamoto and Cecilia Haratani Living Trust.

Appearances: Avid Alavi appeared by zoom for and with Jordan Goldstein (administrator/trustee) by zoom.
Franchesca Callejo (court appointed counsel) appeared by zoom for Jerrold Haratani (conservatee).

Discussion was held regarding Mr. Haratani's inablility to understand the request for increase in the annuity payout, the efforts that are being made to ensure that Mr. Haratani is able to continue to enjoy his baseball games, his shows and his piano lessons while he is still able to do so, the importance of making sure that Mr. Haratani enjoys his life, Mr. Haratani being a very busy man and enjoying his life style, the annuity not having a pay on death beneficiary, Mr. Haratani having a will, etc. The court stated that it approves the investment strategy with Prudent Investor. The Fourth Account and Report of the Successor Conservator of the Estate and Petition for Its Settlement, etc., and the Fourth Account and Report of Successor Trustee and Petition for Its Settlement, etc., are under submission with the court's intent to grant.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Aug-07-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi,Esq., counsel for Jordan Goldstein, Conservator; Franchesca Callejo, Esq., Court-appointed Counsel for the Conservatee; Alan Sparer, Esq., counsel for the Sparer Law Group, former legal counsel for the Conservator.

The Court notes a dispute between the Probate Examiner and Ms. Alavi as the whether an allocation is required between principal and income as to assets received from the trust of the Conservatee's late brother. Ms. Alavi contends that such an allocation would only be required if the Conservatee was solely an income beneficiary of the trust not, in the instant case, where the Conservatee is both an income and principal beneficiary, albeit a contingent principal beneficiary. The Court is inclined to approve the accounting at this time but will require the principal/income allocation in the next accounting.

The Court next notes an unauthorized advance taken by the Conservator and that Mr. Sparer's attorney fee bill was paid without Court authorization. Ms. Alavi acknowledges the same and represents that the same will not happen again.

The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody; Examiner: Sandra Hilton; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not
Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (D204)

MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PCN-09-292920 Conservatorship of Jerrold Haratani and PTR-09-292994 In the Matter of the Maria Miyamoto & Cecilia Haratani Living Trust
Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for (Beneficiary & Conservator) Jerold Haratani. James Curran for (Surety) Platt River Insurance Company.

The Court asked if there was any opposition to the petition for surcharge. Mr. Curran stated yes, that they object to the petitions in both cases, that they are procedurally improper, that the attorney's fees should be a separate petition, that the request for fees was not supported by a declaration, that without a declaration they cannot determine is a surcharge should go against Ms. Witherspoon, that there may be a conflict of interest in Ms. Alavi representing both Ms. Witherspoon and Jordan Goldstein, that it is not clear if Ms. Witherspoon performed the 18 months of service being requested and confirmed that an objection had not been filed. Ms. Callejo stated that she had just been informed of the objection on Friday, that the surety company received the petition weeks ago and that it came as a surprise that they were now objecting, that Ms. Alavi could not be present today but had agreed to provide a declaration, that itemized time sheets have been filed and asked for clarification from the Court as to if it wants separate orders for fees and the surcharge. The Court asked for Mr. Curran's authority regarding fees. Mr. Curran responded that they have no liability until the fees are determined and cited CCP 996.440(b). Ms. Callejo stated that there will be ongoing fees until this is resolved, that they will file a separate petition regarding the fees, that Jordon Goldstein has an accounting coming up and she will have to talk to them, that this may take many months and requested a 30 day continuance for status on filing with an earlier status date for the filing of Objections. The Petition for Surcharge of Fiduciary and Other Remedies (both cases) were continued to March 26, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The objections are to be filed no later than March 12, 2018.

MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-09-292920 Conservatorship of Jerrold Haratani and
PTR-09-292994 The Maria Miyamoto and Cecilia Haratani Living Trust.

Appearances: Avid Alavi for Jordan Goldstein.
Franchesca Callejo Court appointed Counsel for Jerrold Haratani (Conservatee/Beneficiary).
James Curran for Platte River Insurance Company.

Ms. Callejo stated that the Line of Credit has been resolved, that no transactions have been identified to suggest that it was used in an inappropriate way and that there is an issue with respect to Ms. Witherspoon's failure to file income taxes. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not Ms. Witherspoon should be awarded any fees, whether said fees if any can be applied to any surcharge, who should pay for fees and costs incurred due to Ms. Witherspoon's failure to perform her duties and failure to resign, time spent, filings and multiple appearances that were required to do the accounting without supporting documents (bank statements, invoices, time sheets), the inability to determine what time was spent by Ms. Witherspoon for work that she may have performed, whether or not to exonerate the bond, etc. Ms. Alavi stated that she is willing to not be paid for any work the Court feels is the result of the inaction of Ms. Witherspoon, that Ms. Goldstein spent a lot of time on the taxes, that she does not know what Ms. Goldstein's fees are and that she believes Ms. Goldstein will be requesting fees. The Court stated that items that may be candidates for surcharge are any fees Ms. Witherspoon has taken that were not awarded by the Court, costs and fees incurred by Ms. Callejo, costs and fees that that will be sought by Ms. Goldstein and the taxes. The Court allows the Fourth and Final Account and Report of Former Conservator and Former Trustee in both matters without allowing any fees to Ms. Witherspoon (subject to receipt and review of the order). The Court will not exonerate the bond at this time. Ms. Alavi is to revise the order and provide a declaration listing items that they are willing to waive. Status on filing of a petition for surcharge is set for December 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Off Calendar Aug-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for and with Christine Witherspoon.

The Court addressed the examiner's notes regarding services performed that look to be conservator or case manager services, not trustee services. Ms. Alavi reported that Ms. Witherspoon went above and beyond what was expected as a trustee, that she is not requesting extra fees above the 1% guideline fee, that Jeffrey was in need of services and that if she did not assist it would have been a disadvantage to the trust, that Jeffrey had no funds and all of his expenses had to paid out of the trust, that she had to address medical needs as well as issues with his apartment/landlord, that she helped Jeffrey with his personal property to avoid having it placed in a storage unit and that while Ms. Witherspoon provided extra service and took on liability she is not seeking additional fees. The Court stated that it would approve the accounting, and that for the future it is not sure the trustee could not ask for extra fees as it was for the benefit of the trustees and trust, that it appreciates the amount of time spent, that it questions whether it is appropriate to pay from the trust, that it would need more detailed time records and that the request for reduction of bond will also be approved.
